author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The advantage of Portnox CLEAR is that it is very efficient, highly effective, and user-friendly. Its network security features provides high quality protection against viruses and cyber attacks.
cons: The downside of Portnox CLEAR is the very limited monitoring tools, that are essential for managing some aspects of the network. It also is on the higher pricing range.

review_title: Good product to control network access
author: ahmer Z.
pros: Ease of deployment, we were up an running in an hour
cons: Very expensive , the cost per license is high

author: Leo L.
pros: Fully cloud, which removes end user the need to maintain a hardware server. And able to access anywhere.
cons: Rely on 802.1X, which can be hectic if end user doesn't have the relevant knowledge or skill.

review_title: It provide good option for SAAS NAC
description: We succeed to deploy in short time
author: Yaniv L.
pros: 1. Its a SAAS platform. I still can have local radius for case of disconnection 2. I succeed to implement required policy
cons: 1. Nothing I can think about. But I will complete the 40 charchters :)

review_title: Radius Server integration for Wifi Authentication
description: We are using the Radius server for Wifi authentication within our offices. This simplifies the setup and support of a Radius server used for multiple offices within our organization.
author: Martyn B.
pros: Easy to use and implementation. This can be used across multiple sites and easily integrated with AD.
cons: Support is slow to respond on certain occasions.

review_title: Excellent Alternative to the Same Old NAC Solutions
description: The solution is so simple to work with that we figured out 90% of it prior to our onboarding, which by the way was still very helpful. We have the same or better protection that the old solution because Portnox is easier to maintain and configure.
author: Chris B.
pros: It is cloud based, comparatively easy to configure and use, reasonably priced, does not affect PC performance, and has all the features I require in a NAC solution. Requires less time, effort, expense, and expertise to maintain and operate than the typical enterprise level NACs that ruled the segment for so long.
cons: Reviews like this are easy, I can honestly say that we have not run into anything that turns us off. Portnox continues to deliver.

author: Matt B.
pros: Very customizable and was able to authenticate devices that I wasn't expecting it to.
cons: We have had a continued issue where docked laptops are not recognized as domain devices since they come onto the network before portnox is able to scan the computers registry to verify domain relationship. This causes the devices to be dropped from our network. Support has been working on it for several weeks with no clear solution.

description: It's a really good solution and I'd recommend it to anyone
author: Felix M.
pros: It's easy to deploy and manage with minimal resources needed.
cons: Takes a while to scan a new machine once it joins the network...almost a minute
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Eben F.
pros: Web interface is decent. Responsive and well integrated for SSO
cons: Implementation guides etc. are woefully inadequate and don't give nearly enough detail for actual implementation.

author: John O.
pros: Two-factor authentication and threat response features.
cons: Integration with existing systems is a bit hectic.

author: jannie E.
cons: it's not well know to the market and it's difficult to convince customers to move from a Cisco or a Aruba solution.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
